sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Path retracted. Agent is now at the bottom of the corridor (rows 30-35). I need to move UP to align with the blue block (rows 19-22). The agent is 6 rows tall, so to align with blue block at rows 19-22, I need the agent's top to be around row 19. Currently agent is at rows 30-35, so I need to move UP by about 11 rows. That's 2 UP moves (12 rows).
But wait, I need to be more careful. Let me check the exact positions. The agent at the top was at rows 12-17 (aligned with red block at 13-16). The blue block is at rows 19-22. So the agent needs to be at rows 18-23 to align with blue block. That's 6 rows down from the top position. So 1 DOWN move from top, or 2 UP moves from current bottom position.
Actually, let me reconsider. The agent moves in 6-row increments. From the top (rows 12-17), one DOWN moves to rows 18-23 (aligned with blue block at 19-22). Two DOWNs moves to rows 24-29 (aligned with green block at 25-28). Three DOWNs moves to rows 30-35 (current position).
So from current position (3 DOWNs from top), I need to move UP 2 times to get to the blue block position (1 DOWN from top).

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (updated):
- Agent at bottom (3 DOWNs from top), path retracted
- Blue block alignment = 1 DOWN from top = 2 UPs from current
- Green block alignment = 2 DOWNs from top = 1 UP from current

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move UP 2 times to align with blue block, then extend RIGHT
action(['UP', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
